---
title: "Learning Unit Template"
subtitle: "Learning Unit Subtitle"
description: "Learning Unit description (max characters 175)"
author:
  - name: Author One
  - name: Author Two
date: "04/18/2026"
date-modified: now
order: 1
draft: true
image: "assets/img/thumbnail.png"
categories:
  - Unit
---

## Purpose of this page

Use this template to create a single **learning unit** within a learning path.

This page is written for teachers and lecturers who design, deliver, and maintain unit-level e-learning content.

## Draft mode (important)

As long as the document option `draft: true` is set:

- draft units appear when you preview the site
- draft units are blank when you render the site
- draft units do not appear in listings, navigation, the site map, or site search

To publish this unit, remove `draft: true` and render the site.

## Author checklist (before writing)

Complete these items first:

- unit title, subtitle, and short description
- unit order in the learning path (`order`)
- intended audience and prerequisites
- estimated time-on-task
- required resources (files, links, tools)
- related concept/topic pages for cross-linking

## Unit at a glance

### Unit learning outcomes

After completing this unit, students can:

- [Outcome 1]
- [Outcome 2]
- [Outcome 3]

### Prerequisites

- [Required prior knowledge]
- [Recommended prior knowledge]

### Estimated effort

- Preparation: [e.g., 20 minutes]
- Learning activities: [e.g., 45 minutes]
- Follow-up / practice: [e.g., 30 minutes]

## Preparation

Describe what students should do before the main activity.

Include:

- short context and motivation
- required reading or media
- setup instructions (if needed)
- key terms students should know

## Learning activities

Structure the core of the unit into small, clear steps.

### Activity A

- Goal: [What students should achieve]
- Task: [What students do]
- Expected output: [What students submit / produce]

### Activity B

- Goal: [What students should achieve]
- Task: [What students do]
- Expected output: [What students submit / produce]

## Practice and feedback

### Practice tasks

1. [Task 1]
2. [Task 2]
3. [Task 3]

### Feedback guidance

- [Criteria for correct/strong answers]
- [Typical errors and how to correct them]
- [Hints or scaffolding for struggling learners]

## Assessment alignment

Explain how this unit supports the course assessment strategy.

- related assessment components: [quiz / assignment / project / exam]
- evidence of learning: [what demonstrates outcome achievement]
- grading relevance: [formative / summative]

## Links to topics and path sequence

Keep both website views connected:

- Related topics (concept-first): [link to topic pages]
- Previous unit (sequence-first): [link]
- Next unit (sequence-first): [link]

## Teaching notes (for instructors)

### Suggested facilitation

- [How to introduce this unit]
- [Where to pause for discussion]
- [How to run group/individual work]

### Adaptation options

- Large class: [adaptation]
- Small class: [adaptation]
- Online / hybrid delivery: [adaptation]

## Maintenance notes

### Ownership

- Unit owner: [name / role]
- Last pedagogical review: [date]

### Update cycle

- Before semester start: verify links, instructions, and workload
- During semester: fix issues and clarify instructions based on questions
- After semester: revise using feedback and performance data

## Quality gate (before publishing)

- Learning outcomes are measurable and aligned with unit tasks
- Estimated effort is realistic for the target audience
- Activities, practice, and feedback criteria are complete
- Links to previous/next unit and related topics are present
- Language is clear, concise, and learner-friendly
- `draft: true` is removed when the unit is ready to publish
